text:
Once upon a time, there was an old oak tree.

One serene autumn day, a young squirrel named Sammy decided he wanted to climb higher than ever before. He snatched his favorite chestnut shell whistle and dashed up the tree. Higher and higher he went, until he hung gracefully from a large branch.

Suddenly, a wise old owl swooped down. "Careful up there with those beautiful shells! They're filled with stories..." Owl glanced at Sammy. "...but remember, friendship matters more."

Sammy listened closely. From that moment, they became friends. They shared seeds and stories. Sammy liked to tell tales about distant places like Africa.

One evening, after nights filled with song birds singing sweet melodies, Owl suggested they fly south for winter. Sammy was thrilled. They swapped tales until the sun settled low behind the heavens.

As they slept under a thick blanket of stars, Owl whispered a promise. "Remember how you love listening? Just like that."

When winter storms shook them hard
